objectives: Identify core building blocks across major cloud providers and how they map to common workloads
Explain fundamental cloud concepts and service categories used in modern architectures
learning_path.title: Cloud Foundations: Core Services and Cloud Technologies Overview

objectives: Apply common cloud architecture patterns to meet scalability, reliability, and cost goals
Produce a high-level target architecture for a sample application
learning_path.title: Designing Cloud Architecture with Patterns

objectives: Perform essential Linux administration tasks commonly required for cloud-hosted systems
Troubleshoot basic OS-level issues impacting application availability
learning_path.title: Linux Administration for Cloud Workloads

objectives: Design least-privilege access using roles, policies, and RBAC concepts
Explain encryption-at-rest/in-transit approaches and key management using KMS
learning_path.title: Identity, Access, and Key Management in the Cloud

objectives: Create and manage cloud infrastructure using declarative IaC workflows
Implement safe change management practices for infrastructure updates
learning_path.title: Infrastructure as Code: Repeatable Cloud Environments

objectives: Build container images and run containers locally for development and testing
Apply basic container best practices for configuration and security
learning_path.title: Containers with Docker: Packaging and Running Applications

objectives: Deploy and operate containerized workloads on a managed Kubernetes service
Explain core Kubernetes objects and how they support scaling and resilience
learning_path.title: Kubernetes Fundamentals on Managed Services (EKS/AKS/GKE)

objectives: Design a CI/CD pipeline that builds, tests, and deploys to cloud environments
Implement deployment strategies that reduce risk and improve release reliability
learning_path.title: CI/CD Pipelines for Cloud Deployments

objectives: Design serverless components for common application patterns
Compare serverless tradeoffs and integrate with other cloud services
learning_path.title: Serverless Architectures: Event-Driven Compute

objectives: Select appropriate storage types based on access patterns, consistency, and cost
Design data storage layouts for durability, performance, and security
learning_path.title: Data Storage in the Cloud: SQL, NoSQL, and Object Storage

objectives: Instrument services to collect logs, metrics, and traces for troubleshooting and performance analysis
Define actionable alerts and dashboards aligned to service objectives
learning_path.title: Observability: Logging, Metrics, and Tracing

objectives: Create monitoring and reporting views for capacity, cost, and reliability tracking
Establish operational reporting routines to support continuous improvement
learning_path.title: Cloud Monitoring and Reporting for Operations

objectives: Design for fault tolerance using multi-zone and multi-region approaches
Define DR targets and implement recovery strategies aligned to business requirements
learning_path.title: High Availability and Disaster Recovery Design

matched_occupation.description: Cloud engineers are responsible for the design, planning, management and maintenance of cloud-based systems. They develop and implement cloud-applications, handle the migration of existing on-premise applications to the cloud, and debug cloud stacks.
matched_occupation.label: cloud engineer
